title: HTTP authentication enabled by default when ADMIN_PUBLIC_BASE_URL is HTTP
source: Unauthenticated Internet client connecting to the panel over HTTP or a deployment that omits ALLOW_INSECURE_HTTP_AUTH.

root_control.detail: resolve_http_auth_setting defaults omitted flag to true for non-HTTPS ADMIN_PUBLIC_BASE_URL.

sink.detail: auth_transport_is_allowed accepts ALLOW_INSECURE_HTTP_AUTH for login, recovery, and refresh routes.

impact: Credential, access-cookie, and refresh-cookie exposure to network interception or modification.

title: Unauthenticated treasury history exposes player actors and transaction details

root_control.detail: Public projections read actor, details, amount, timestamps, owner identifiers and return them.

sink.detail: Public status, budget history, and treasury routes invoke the projections without an auth dependency.

impact: Cross-account financial activity and player-identifying metadata are enumerable by the public Internet.

title: Non-atomic refresh-token rotation permits concurrent replay
source: Attacker who obtains a valid refresh cookie and can issue concurrent requests.

root_control.detail: Refresh token is verified and a replacement pair is issued before the old JTI is revoked; no compare-and-set or lock is visible.

sink.detail: Admin and player refresh endpoints call the rotation routine.

impact: A stolen refresh token can be replayed in parallel to mint multiple valid refresh sessions, extending persistence and complicating revocation.

title: Release auth patch extracts a root-selected archive without member containment checks
source: Operator or automation supplies a crafted archive path to the sudo-oriented release auth patch script.

root_control.detail: Archive path is accepted and passed to tar with no member validation.

sink.detail: tar writes archive members directly into WORK_ROOT before payload discovery.

impact: Traversal or link members can overwrite files outside WORK_ROOT in the invoking privilege context and influence plugin/service startup.

title: Ubuntu auth patch extracts a root archive without member containment checks
source: Operator or automation supplies a crafted archive and matching sidecar to the EUID-0 deployment script.

root_control.detail: Root check and SHA256 sidecar are present, but archive member names and link types are not validated.

sink.detail: tar writes archive members directly into WORK_ROOT.

impact: A malicious or compromised archive can escape staging while the script is root and alter root-owned configuration, code, or service inputs.

title: Release unpack-and-verify script trusts archive member paths
source: Caller supplies a crafted release archive to the unpack-and-verify workflow.

root_control.detail: Only tar/zip readability is checked; no member containment or link policy is applied.

impact: Archive traversal or links can write outside the staging root in the invoking user or deployment privilege context.

title: Full replacement installer extracts a checked-but-unconfined archive
source: Caller supplies a release archive to the full replacement installer, optionally with a matching sidecar.

root_control.detail: Readability and optional SHA256 are checked, but members and links are not inspected.

impact: A crafted archive can escape staging before full replacement service/filesystem operations and alter deployment files.

title: Ubuntu rollback extracts an unconfined backup archive
source: Caller supplies a backup archive to the root rollback command.

root_control.detail: tar listing is used as a readability check only; no member or link containment policy is applied.

sink.detail: tar extracts directly into stage_root before the project-tree assertion.

impact: Traversal/link members can overwrite files outside stage_root in root context before rollback proceeds.

title: PowerShell release validator extracts an unconfined archive
source: Caller supplies ArchivePath to the Windows release validator.

root_control.detail: Hash and bootstrap manifest checks do not validate archive member names or links.

sink.detail: tar extracts directly into a temporary directory.

impact: A crafted archive can escape the temporary directory in the validator's Windows user/elevated execution context, enabling workstation or supply-chain impact.

title: /api/runtime trusts loopback source address behind a reverse proxy
source: External caller reaches the panel through a reverse proxy that presents a loopback client address.

root_control.detail: Panel-admin authentication is skipped solely when is_loopback_request is true.

sink.detail: Response includes startup report and managed runtime snapshot.

impact: Unauthenticated callers may learn internal project/app paths, artifact locations, release metadata, and startup diagnostics useful for reconnaissance.
